subject: Human Resources
title: Alumni Networks
description: Learn Alumni Networks in one minute.

hook.text: McKinsey, Google, and Goldman Sachs maintain active alumni networks. Former employees become clients and referrals.

buildup.text: Alumni networks keep former employees connected to the company through events, newsletters, and communities.

discovery.text: About 15% of new hires at top companies are 'boomerang' employees—people who left and came back.

twist.text: The cost of re-hiring an alum is far lower than hiring a stranger. They already know the culture and systems.

climax.text: Smart companies treat departure as a pause, not an ending—keeping the door open for future collaboration.

quiz.question: What is a boomerang employee?
options: Someone who leaves a company and later returns
An employee who works at two companies
